description_long: This is a large, rustic barn structure with a complex, intersecting roof system featuring both gable and hip elements. The exterior is clad in horizontal wooden siding with a stone foundation visible at the base. The roof is primarily covered in brown shingles, with a prominent section of red corrugated metal roofing over one wing. The front features a wide, open porch supported by wooden posts, and there are several large barn doors and windows distributed across the facade. The building has a spacious, open interior suitable for agricultural storage or livestock, and a chimney is visible, suggesting a heated interior space.
description: A large, rustic barn with intersecting gable and hip roofs, horizontal wood siding, stone foundation, and a mix of shingle and metal roofing. Features include a wide porch, multiple barn doors, and several windows.
description_view_0: The barn is seen from above and slightly to the front, highlighting the intersecting rooflines and the red metal roof section.
description_view_1: This view shows the main entrance with a wide porch, central gable, and visible support posts, as well as the main barn doors.
description_view_2: The side of the barn is visible, showing the horizontal siding, stone foundation, and the length of the red metal roof.
description_view_3: The rear of the barn is shown, with the red metal roof section and a covered porch area, as well as the chimney.
attribute_roof_type: Intersecting gable and hip roof with a section of shed roof covered in metal panels
attribute_siding_pattern: Horizontal lap siding with a stone base
attribute_door_style: Double hinged barn doors at the main entrance, with additional single hinged doors on the sides
attribute_window_arrangement: Several windows distributed irregularly, with a large window on the right wing and smaller windows on other facades
attribute_architectural_style: Traditional American barn with rustic lodge influences
